This modest, 1960s semi-detached house might go unnoticed by those who pass it, but its run-down exterior tells the story of thousands of forgotten homes across Britain. The Hampshire property has quietly sat still for more than six decades, with only rats scurrying across its interior and foxes frequently visiting its overgrown garden. The lonely house is one of 265,000 long-term empty residential homes across the country that have been vacant for more than six months, according to Empty Property Hunters.

My mum had a hostess trolley it was such a good idea because every Sunday lunch she would do all the preparation and we'd wash up all the pans and then she would relax with a sherry before serving a piping hot dinner , and my dad would slice the meat with his electric knife ( making sure he saved enough meat for a sandwich for his tea ) !

From airing cupboards to electric knives, hostess trolleys and electric can openers, middle-class British homes were inundated in the 80s and 90s with new technology. Homes were also filled with smart built-in features such as pulley maids and the trusty kitchen to dining room hatch, which allowed one to pass food items at ease and speed. But now, many of these savvy home gadgets and features have faded into existence.

Brits have lost millions of pounds trying to sell retirement properties over the last decade as their values plunge due to high service charges and restrictive leases. Many of the UK's 190,000 privately owned retirement flats are now worth far less than when they were built with an extreme example having fallen by 95 per cent. Frustrated pensioners have watched service charges soar by more than a half in just seven years on their homes, making them difficult to sell and leaving some empty.

  • Her appearance is a manifestation of the hurt and pain she is clearly feeling inside. Trauma can profoundly alter a person's perception of themselves and, in some cases lead to behaviours or physical changes which make them feel ugly, undesirable or appear less conventionally attractive. It is a protective mechanism. Psychologically this often stems from self disgust, shame and a desire to regain control over their bodies and minds. You might think comments making fun of her are harmless, but just consider how it would feel to be in such turmoil.

  • Radacanu was never as good as she was made out to be. She had the tournament of her life with the 2021 US Open combined with a huge slice of luck in not having to play a single top 10 player throughout the entire tournament. You can only beat what's in front of you, as the tired old cliche goes, but there are two other hugely damning stats. Firstly, she's never won another tournament since. Hasn't even reached a final. Secondly, she's only ever beaten three top ten players in her entire career. Face it, Emma is a top 50 player at best who has been convinced - whether by herself or by others - that she's more. And every time it goes wrong, it couldn't possibly be own lack of ability and it must be the coach's fault. I'll be amazed if she ever has a deep run in a grand slam again.

Religion causes misery for the majority of people on this planet.

Huge numbers of Muslims prayed on Mount Arafat on Tuesday at the climax of the hajj pilgrimage, as a punishing desert sun sent temperatures soaring towards 40C. From daybreak, thousands of white-robed worshippers recited Quranic verses on the 230 feet rocky hill near Mecca, where the Prophet Mohammed is believed to have delivered his last sermon. More than 1.5 million people have joined the hajj this year despite the shadow of war across the Middle East created by the US and Israeli war with Iran.

  • Saw him live at Croke Park. One man in a plaid shirt with the guitar and some type of foot operated device where he could control every other musical instrument he had pre-recorded himself. No backing band no fancy lights just Ed. 80,000 people absolutely glued and the atmosphere was electric. Amazing night. But yeah, armchair critics who clearly know nothing about music will call him overated, the tens of thousands of people who fill out stadiums all around the world to see him clearly know nothing about music, right?

  • I reluctantly selected the documentary to watch last night, not being a fan, but like most, was there when each record was released. Gosh, I never knew just how gruelling her schedule was and how hard she worked, at a very young age receiving deriding criticism. Then late on in the documentary, she candidly discusses her cancer diagnosis and treatment, how it affected her personal life and her career. This was the point I could identify with Kylie. I too was diagnosed with breast cancer and endured the gruelling treatment. 20 months later, and I'm still not the person I was, no stamina, no energy and in pain. I never thought I'd say this about Kylie, but she deserves all the accolades, all the adulation. She's more than earned it.

Daily Mail trying to take credit for Dracula now lol.

An apparent friendship between Dracula writer Bram Stoker and a Daily Mail journalist may have helped play a key role in promoting the famous vampire novel, the author's family have revealed. Robert Leighton, the newspaper's late 19th-century literary editor, sent a 'quite personal' letter to Stoker - highlighted as 'remarkable' by one of the Irish writer's relatives. Dacre Stoker, who has researched and written extensively about his great, great uncle, highlighted the correspondence from Leighton to Bram in late May 1897, just before Dracula was published.
  • 27 comments
  • share Friendship between Dracula author and Mail man helped novel's success

Analysis of official birth statistics suggests more babies are born nine months after the May bank holiday than at any other time of year.

Why do they always build these hot springs next to tourist attractions..

It was designated as the first national park in the United States in 1872 and is home to some of the most distinctive geology in the world - including a supervolcano which could cause a mass extinction event on eruption. But with all that potential danger, Yellowstone is still a bucket list place to visit for its outstanding natural beauty.
  • 17 comments
  • share Photo shows the sheer size of one of the world's largest hot springs

  • Radacanu was never as good as she was made out to be. She had the tournament of her life with the 2021 US Open combined with a huge slice of luck in not having to play a single top 10 player throughout the entire tournament. You can only beat what's in front of you, as the tired old cliche goes, but there are two other hugely damning stats. Firstly, she's never won another tournament since. Hasn't even reached a final. Secondly, she's only ever beaten three top ten players in her entire career. Face it, Emma is a top 50 player at best who has been convinced - whether by herself or by others - that she's more. And every time it goes wrong, it couldn't possibly be own lack of ability and it must be the coach's fault. I'll be amazed if she ever has a deep run in a grand slam again.
